name: Limited Visibility
description: A horror visibility constraint that limits what the player can see through darkness, fog, camera framing, occlusion, blindness, or unreliable light.
design_notes: Limited visibility should shape uncertainty without hiding required information unfairly. Designers need to separate navigation readability, threat telegraphing, puzzle readability, and aesthetic darkness. The system should define how visibility interacts with light resources, monster detection, accessibility brightness, and checkpoint restore state.
design_purpose: Create vulnerability by narrowing what the player can confidently inspect.
Make light, sound, map knowledge, and route planning matter.
Control reveal timing for threats, clues, exits, and environmental storytelling.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are pushing into darkness where danger may be close but not fully knowable.

parameters.name: visibility_radius
parameters.description: Playable sight radius before darkness or fog obscures details.

parameters.name: darkness_band_id
parameters.description: Lighting band that drives readability and fear-state effects.

parameters.name: occlusion_profile_id
parameters.description: Visual constraint profile applied to the current area.

parameters.name: threat_reveal_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule controlling how threats become readable.

parameters.name: accessibility_brightness_floor
parameters.description: Minimum allowed scene luminance for readable play.

parameters.name: checkpoint_restore_state
parameters.description: Visibility state restored after checkpoint or save load.

edge_cases: A required pickup, exit, or puzzle symbol is outside the readable visibility area.
A monster attacks from darkness before any audio or silhouette telegraph can play.
Accessibility brightness settings reveal hidden navigation blockers or stealth cues.
A checkpoint restores the player into blackout while the nearest light source is empty.
Multiplayer spectators or split-screen cameras reveal areas the active player should not see.
common_bugs: Post-processing darkness hides UI prompts, subtitles, or accessibility indicators.
AI perception uses full scene visibility while the player cannot see the same threat.
Fog and darkness settings stack multiplicatively and make the scene unreadable on some displays.
Threat reveal state resets on camera cuts and repeats the same reveal cue.
Brightness sliders affect menus and gameplay together, making either UI or darkness hard to read.
balancing_notes: Darkness is more effective when the player still has enough information to choose movement, hiding, or retreat.
Visibility constraints should vary by area and pacing beat so players do not become numb to constant darkness.
Revealing silhouettes before detail can preserve fear while reducing unfair surprise attacks.
accessibility_notes: Provide brightness, contrast, reduced vignette, and reduced blur settings.
Keep subtitles, interaction prompts, and critical UI outside heavy darkness filters.
Offer non-visual threat cues for players who cannot rely on subtle silhouettes.

implementation_notes.unity: Drive visibility through a controller that owns visibility_radius, darkness_band_id, and occlusion_profile_id. Apply post-processing, light masks, and camera settings from that state, and keep UI canvases outside gameplay darkness unless intentionally filtered.
implementation_notes.godot: Use CanvasModulate, Light2D, Environment settings, fog, or shader masks while storing darkness state in an area controller. Emit changes to AI perception and accessibility settings so detection and player readability stay aligned.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Post Process Volumes, lighting channels, fog, and camera settings with explicit Gameplay Tags for darkness bands. AI perception should query the same area state when deciding whether the player or monster can be perceived.
implementation_notes.web: For Canvas or Phaser, render visibility masks separately from gameplay collision and UI layers. In Three.js, keep fog and postprocessing parameters tied to serialized area state so reloads restore the intended visibility band.

example_games.title: Amnesia: The Dark Descent
example_games.note: Uses darkness and limited visibility to increase vulnerability.

example_games.title: Outlast
example_games.note: Uses limited visual tools and darkness during exploration and pursuit.
